title: helianthemum apenninum f. stoechadifolium
intro: Helianthemum apenninum f. stoechadifolium, also known as Rock Rose, is a flowering plant species within the Helianthemum genus, characterized by its showy pink flowers and woody stems. Traditionally, Rock Rose has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent effects. The health benefits of Rock Rose include its ability to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory problems. Rock Rose is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
